slides.title: MoQ Performance and Feedback Requirements for Live Media and Agent Interaction

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: MoQ over MoQ :-) https://moq.conf.meetecho.com/ietf126-moq/

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Lorenzo Miniero said:

MoQ over MoQ :-) https://moq.conf.meetecho.com/ietf126-moq/

Screenshot 2026-07-23 at 10.32.34.png
works here!

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: Will is maybe overloud in the room, he's hard to make out

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: Oh it's a PR - I was looking for a draft to review

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: @Mike English :partying_face:

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: @Jonathan Lennox I'll see if we can reduce the gain

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Better now?

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Yes on the stage

chat.author: Ali Begen
chat.text: yup

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text: yes in the room

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: Did I just hear that the limit protection recourse is session wide? Tear the whole session down if too much is asked? Or is the idea that we'd have more granular errors? (Thinking more about what all of these limits look like in practice for applications)

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Mike you likely should join the queue for this.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: +1 The editors are here to reflect the consensus of the working group

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: I am confused, I clearly remember us agreeing to a draft at a virtual interim

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: The consensus call conclusion in the email says draft. I have to refresh if our minutes actually from London says anything else.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: +1 Cullen. We should be consistent with the process.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: My preference would be (as previously stated) that we should take on work like this, but as extension drafts.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: +1 Alan - bigger features like this are easier to review as separate drafts, imo, too

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: I might not have been paying close attention, but is there a "use the best algorithm up to server's discretion" option?

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: If people think this is totally done and ready to land, we could land it in the base draft, but I'm generally wary of features we don't have much implementation experience with yet coming in at this stage and then burdening the base draft with extra complexity and iteration required to polish them with implementation experience.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: No I don't think so.  I think the current mechanism is publisher offers, subscriber chooses

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: So Alan, do you want Will to compose a separate draft before we have a consensus call?

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: When this was called DTS there already was a separate draft, so I'm confused on how we ended up with a PR.

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: Or for that matter, do others need to see this in draft form to evaluate?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: I think the question is: is there consensus to merge this PR now?  If the answer is no, it needs to be in a draft (which we should immediately adopt, in my opinion)

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text:
PR says 
The default value is any empty list which prohibits the use of SSTS.

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text: so no need to do 0 if it turns out to be not usefull

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: (still on my extension draft soapbox) I also still think there's value in exercising our extensibility mechanisms

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: +1 Mike

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: +1 for adoption.. in a separate draft :)

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: I'm not wholly opposed to having the useful stub in the base draft. I'd have to see the PR for that to say for sure, but maybe that's a useful compromise.

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text: You could define an algorithm called 1 which was "do whatever you think is best"

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: Are algorithms in the order of server prference?

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: But it does raise questions about whether that's necessary and what that might mean for later (post MoQT v1) extensions

chat.author: Victor Vasiliev
chat.text: *preference

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: Victor is echoing my 'server knows best' idea

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: (I think)

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: To paint the bikeshed, I'd number the "do what you think best" 0 and "simple algorithm" 1

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: I'm concerned about the idea that it'll be hard to extend MoQT without having stubs or carve outs in the base draft because what does that mean after we ship it? Closing the door on certain classes of extensibility?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: It's "the next object larger than largest object" - there's an open issue I think about this

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Mike, I think you can do any type of extensions, one only have to appropriately specify how it affect existing procedures. I interpret Cullen's objections that he just finds it hard to see how it would interact with what is included.

chat.author: Will Law
chat.text: @Alan - I changed the text in the PR to reflect this and to implement the language you proposed.

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Removing the non-fill versions in favor of the fill versions of Absolute was the outcome of a London discussion

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: @will - i was replying to Mo's comment about "NextObject"

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: ("hashtag no filter")

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Magnus Westerlund said:

Mike, I think you can do any type of extensions, one only have to appropriately specify how it affect existing procedures. I interpret Cullen's objections that he just finds it hard to see how it would interact with what is included.

OK, so it's just a readability issue then? That's less concerning. Once MoQT v1 is a stable and fixed thing, it'll likely be easier to reason about modifications to its behavior by extensions so maybe stubbing things out makes sense now, but we won't require that later?

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Mike that is at least my personal view.

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: My personal opinion is the specific arrangement of drafts doesn't really matter for the end product, within reason, but does matter a lot for workflow, as once it's in MOQT issues that come from implementation go into the editors' funnel, rather than an author, and therefore ultimately delay moqt

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: OTOH, spending 15 min arguing about it every session is also delaying MOQT

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: I'm also hearing mixed signals on whether a subsystem like SSTS is easier to grok as a PR or a draft

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Martin Duke said:

My personal opinion is the specific arrangement of drafts doesn't really matter for the end product, within reason, but does matter a lot for workflow, as once it's in MOQT issues that come from implementation go into the editors' funnel, rather than an author, and therefore ultimately delay moqt

100% this. I'd like to decouple the development lifecycle of new big features from the base draft at this point, if we can.

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: (Am I the only one who keeps wondering who Phil is?)

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: It would also help figure out how robust negotiation of optional features is

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Sorry Jonathan - I had a slide explaining 1673 in Monday's talk

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: but forgot to update it

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: No, it's fine, my brain is just mis-parsing the phonemes.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Jonathan Lennox said:

(Am I the only one who keeps wondering who Phil is?)

Is Phil's group called Gensis?

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: :drum:

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: The Prince of Insufficient Light?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Is anyone against this?  Seems fine unless anyone has objections

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: I need to review, but seems sensible to me

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text:
Ian Swett said:

I need to review, but seems sensible to me

ditto

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text: There are a lot of comments in the PR

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Cullen: 1673 or Location Filters?

chat.author: Suhas Nandakumar
chat.text: same as Ian on it

chat.author: 八谷航太
chat.text: Suppose a client sends REQUEST_UPDATE(forward=1) with location_filter specifying future obejcts, will SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_UPDATE be sent at the time the target object is generated and transmitted?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: No, it's only when not in response to REQUEST_UDPATE

chat.author: 八谷航太
chat.text: Ok that makes sense

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: PUBLISH_NOTIFY is fine.  #shipit

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: Terrible idea...you're publishing updates about a subscription which clients can choose to receive.  Meta-track?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Jonathan, I guess we could have the subscriber opt-out

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: It just feels like the shape here is moq about moq

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: it's not quite that bad I don't think.  Making another track for this seems like overkill, and the perf/memory implications may be not great

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: it's just in the bidi stream

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text: +1 Martin that we need a table

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: there's already an issue about adding a chart of params

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: please don't file another one

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Copying parameters is what I do too, I think, so I agree. Knowing which SUBSCRIBE_TRACKS a PUBLISH came from may be useful anyway, though: maybe just a reference to the SUBSCRIBE_TRACKS request_id could be enough

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Martin where's your MOQ Transport footgun logo

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: https://quicdev.slack.com/archives/C046V0QF3CK/p1781272186898699

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: 3 digit issue - boom

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: Alan are you updating GitHub with each conclusion?

chat.author: Alan Frindell
chat.text: Oh, usually Ian does that :/

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: I am confident he is not

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Isn't "not knowing the exact cause" common to plenty of errors?

chat.author: Manish
chat.text: why would an error have multiple tokens?

chat.author: Suhas Nandakumar
chat.text: error dosn't have multiple tokens

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: Maybe something good can come from the reason phrase

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Martin +1

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Would at least help if the main issue is logging

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: redoing the REQUEST_ERROR format just for this seems sad

chat.author: Lorenzo Miniero
chat.text: Not really something you can use programmatically but at least "something"

chat.author: Suhas Nandakumar
chat.text: i think we have auth paramter to have index , then echong that index in error seems ok too ?

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: to avoid a whole new request-error format, I would recommend either (a) a range of error codes that allows an integer reference, or (b) use the reason phrase

chat.author: Jonathan Lennox
chat.text: I don't see why you'd need to convert an arbitrary https uri to moqt -- most http isn't moq

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: I'd love to further clarify moqt:// requirements, but it does interact with how we define scopes, so it is kind of hairy

chat.author: Ian Swett
chat.text: New versions of MoQT "Will describe how they expect to interact with exsiting extensions."

chat.author: Christian Huitema
chat.text: Cullen just implicitly assumed that version 1 will one day come out! There is hope!
